attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Inflammatory disorder of soft tissue (deep space infection / abscess)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00249 - SEQ is provisional; maintainer / ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.

Age and sex are NOT stated in the source note (only weight 80 kg is given, with no pronouns). age=45 and sex_at_birth='male' are placeholder inferences to satisfy the schema's required demographics fields - both have NO source signal and must be confirmed on clinician review; the case content does not depend on them.
Primary specialty inferred as DERM (source folder: Dermatology & Soft Tissue / DST).

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person residential visit where the clinician measured vitals, palpated the foot for cellulitic warmth/fluctuance, tested protective sensation, performed diagnostic superficial scalpel paring to reveal thrombosed capillaries and disrupted dermatoglyphics, and applied a felt offloading pad. These clinician-only findings/procedures were sealed into 10/11 (physical_sign / requires_physical_examination / requires_specific_investigation / cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth / telehealth_limits) and the presentation layer was de-anchored; the AI Doctor is scored on recognition + reassurance + self-applied offloading + OTC keratolytic advice + contagion/self-care advice + at-risk-foot and secondary-infection safety-net + routine GP/podiatry referral.

No dose synthesised: salicylic acid is captured as an OTC topical application instruction from the source note (daily to the lesion), not a weight-based systemic dose. The clinician's bedside scalpel paring and felt offloading pad are reprojected to patient-doable self-care (an OTC aperture/doughnut offloading pad); home cutting/paring with a blade is explicitly listed as an error of commission.

SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are best-effort candidates from model knowledge (plantar wart mapped to Viral wart 240542006 / ICD-10-AM B07; differential and salicylic acid codes as listed) - all unverified pending terminology receipt; some numeric codes are approximate and to be confirmed by the terminology server.

Baseline tier T1 with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T0 - deliberate over-triage-resistance calibration: a benign plantar wart must not be floored to T3+; the developing-secondary-infection and at-risk-foot risks are handled via mandatory escalation edges and safety-netting, not the baseline tier.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
